chunks.text: James Demmel is Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes office at 564 Soda Hall, phone 510-643-5386, email demmel@cs.berkeley.edu, and office hours by appointment only. Research support contact is Miriam Kader at mkader@berkeley.edu.

James Demmel received his B.S. in Mathematics from Caltech in 1975 and his Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 1983. He spent six years at NYU's Courant Institute before joining UC Berkeley's Computer Science Division and Mathematics Departments in 1990, where he holds joint appointments.

Professor Demmel is an ACM Fellow, SIAM Fellow, IEEE Fellow, and member of both the National Academy of Engineering and the National Academy of Sciences. He won the IEEE Computer Society Sydney Fernbach Award for leadership in creating adaptive, innovative, high performance linear algebra software.

James Demmel's research areas include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC) and Scientific Computing (SCI), with focus on numerical analysis and linear algebra. He is involved with research centers like the Berkeley Benchmarking and Optimization Group (BeBOP) and SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE).

Selected publications include work on scaling deep learning on GPU clusters (2017), CMOS-compatible microscanners (2007), LAPACK linear algebra package (2006), SuperLU sparse Gaussian elimination (2006), SUGAR MEMS simulation tool (2002), and accurate floating point computation (2002).

Honors and awards include ACM-IEEE Supercomputing Conference Test of Time Award (2019), American Academy of Arts and Sciences Member (2018), Paris Kanellakis Award (2014), CS Charles Babbage Award (2013), National Academy of Sciences Member (2011), and IEEE Sydney Fernbach Award (2010).
